text: Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ nan na wʋ kara nɩ tɩn, o dɩ yɩ joori sɔŋɔ sɩ o kwe o gɔrɔ o zʋ.
translated: The child who is not taught by his father will later regret that he did not listen to his father's words.

ref: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
text: Zezi ma pa nɔɔna zanzan na yazurǝ dɩ ba yawɩɩrʋ dwi dwi, yɩ o daarɩ o zǝli ciciri zanzan nɔɔna yɩra nɩ. O ma wʋ sɛ sɩ ciciri sɩm ŋɔɔnɩ kʋlʋkʋlʋ, sɩ na maŋɩ sɩ ye o na yɩ wʋlʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.
translated: If a person has a wound on their leg and it is not treated, it will eventually develop into a sore. If they then apply medicine to the sore, it will heal and the person will get better.

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: O na je da kʋntʋ tɩn, kɔ-fɔrɔ maa tui o te, yɩ ba jaanɩ yawɩɩna zanzan ba wǝli da ba tui. Badaara dɛɛn yɩ gwanɩ, badonnǝ maa yɩ lilwǝ dɩ kwaarʋ dɩ mɩmaarʋ, kʋ wǝli dɩ balʋ yawɩɩrʋ dwi dwi na jɩgɩ-ba tɩn. Ba maa jaanɩ-ba ba ve ba tiŋi Zezi yigǝ nɩ, yɩ o pa ba na yazurǝ.
translated: If a person has a wound, and a stranger comes to see him, they will not immediately start talking about his sickness. First they will greet him, then they will ask about the welfare of his family, and after that they will slowly come to speak about his sickness. They will not go straight to the point of telling him he is going to die.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, á yɩ taá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ, sɩ abam dɩdʋa dɩdʋa tiini á jɩgɩ kuri á dwe kabǝmbǝli zanzan.
translated: When the sun rises, it is not yet hot, but as it gradually climbs higher it becomes very hot and burns fiercely.

ref: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
text: Tɩga na tu ka kwɩɩrɩ tɩn, nɔɔna maa tɔɔlɩ ba wɩ: ‹Kan-barʋ wʋm maa bɩɩna! Zaŋɩ-na á nuŋi á jeer-o.›
translated: When we got to the river bank, the man said to them: “The Kan-barʋ has come and is angry! Take me and throw me into the water.”

ref: “Come on!” Jesus said. Peter then got out of the boat and started walking on the water toward him.
text: Zezi maa wɩ, o ba. Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma nuŋi naboro kʋm wʋnɩ o tu na bam baŋa nɩ o ve o maa jeeri Zezi.
translated: Zezi’s mother said, “My child, I have been looking for you everywhere. I didn’t know where you had gone, and I was worried about you, Zezi.”

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: Ba na tagɩ ba wɩ, cicirǝ mʋ jɩg-o tɩn, mʋ o tagɩ kʋntʋ dɩ ba. Zezi nu dɩ Zezi nyaana na yɩ balʋ tɩn (Matiyu
translated: When they had gone, the angel appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up, take the child and his mother and flee to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod is going to search for the child to kill him.” (Matthew

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
text: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm daa ta nyɩ dɩ pipimpiinu na beeri o lagɩ kandwa-ŋʋna sɩ o yǝgi te tɩn.
translated: They said that if the child does not listen to the elders, he will go astray.

ref: The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Instructions for the Twelve Apostles (Mark
text: Simɔn wʋlʋ na yɩ Zelɔtɩ nɔɔna bam wʋ nɔɔnʋ tɩn, dɩ Zudasɩ Yiskarɩyo wʋlʋ na wʋ́ ba o yǝgi Zezi o pa nɔɔna tɩn.
translated: Simon was one of the Zealots, and Judas Iscariot was the one who betrayed Jesus.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Awo. Abam wʋrʋ na ba lana yɩ á ta ye sɩ á taá paɩ á biǝ wo-laarʋ tɩlʋ ba na lagɩ tɩn, á Ko Wɛ na lamma DƖ dwe abam tɩn bá kwe wo-laarʋ DƖ pa balʋ na loori-DƖ tɩn na?
translated: Now. The elders said that if the chief tells you to go and fetch water for him to drink, and you refuse, will Ko Wɛ and the ancestors of DƖ agree that you should fetch water for the car-DƖ to drink?

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: Zezi ma joori o vu sɔŋɔ. Kɔgɔ zanzan daa ta ma ba kʋ gilimi sɔŋɔ kʋm. Kʋ ma pa Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam daa warɩ ba na laŋa sɩ ba di wʋdiu dɩ.
translated: They seized Zezi and took him to prison. The next morning they brought him out to be hanged. But Zezi did not agree that they should kill him, so they left him and went away.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Kʋ daarɩ, abam na wʋ yagɩ á ma cɛ balʋ na kɩ abam lwarɩm tɩn, á Ko Wɛ dɩ bá yagɩ abam lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ abam.
translated: When the time comes, if they want to take the child and go to the river to perform the river rite, they will take the child to the river on the day that Ko We has set for the river rite.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá sɛ o tarɩgɩ kanɩa o pu tɩtɔgɔ kuri nɩ. Oó kwe-ka o palɩ wɛɛnɩ mʋ, sɩ ka pooni dɩm taa naɩ dɩ paɩ nɔɔna balʋ maama na wʋ digǝ kam wʋnɩ tɩn.
translated: Every day he goes to the farm he kills a grasscutter. He says that if he had known, he would have taken it home so that the children could eat it, because it is better for the children to eat it than for it to rot in the bush.

ref: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
text: Ciciri sɩm ma loori Zezi sɩ wɩ: «Pa dɩbam cwǝŋǝ sɩ dɩ́ vu dɩ́ zʋ tǝrǝ bam.»
translated: The bird said to the car Zezi: "Don't run over the child, but rather run over the stone."

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Zan karabiǝ bam na lwarɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn, ba ma zaŋɩ ba vu ba kwe o yɩra yam ba ja vu ba kɩ. Zezi na pɛ kɔ-fɔrɔ di wʋdiu ba su te tɩn (Matiyu
translated: When evening came, the disciples came to him and said, “This is a remote place, and it’s already getting late. Send the crowds away, so they can go to the villages and buy themselves some food.” (Matthew

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
text: Balʋ na jɩgɩ ban-pʋnʋ tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, Wɛ na wʋ́ pa ba taa te lʋgʋ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.
translated: The child who does not listen to advice, his mother will not give him food to eat.

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Zezi laan ma pa o karabiǝ bam ni, sɩ ba pa nɔɔna bam pɔɔrɩ kɔgɔ kɔgɔ ba jǝni tɩga nɩ ga-lɩʋ kʋm baŋa nɩ.
translated: If they had not gone to the farm, they would not have met the stranger who came to ask them for directions.

ref: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
text: «Yɩ zaŋɩ n ta kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn n brɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ. Nan yɔɔrɩ n vu Wɛ kaanɩm tu wʋm te, sɩ n pa o nii n yɩra yam, sɩ n daarɩ n kwe wǝǝnu tɩlʋ Moyisi cullu tɩm na brɩ nɩ tɩ maŋɩ tɩn n pa-o, sɩ o ma o kaanɩ Baŋa-Wɛ. Kʋntʋ wʋ́ brɩ nɔɔna maama nɩ n yawɩʋ kʋm sɩɩnɩ kʋ je.»
translated: They took me to the river to wash me clean. But when they reached the water, they said I should not enter, because they had heard that Moses had commanded that no one should enter that water, saying it belongs to the River-god. So they left me there and went home.

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: O laan ma tʋŋɩ-ba sɩ ba vu Betelɩhɛm, yɩ o wɩ: «Ve-na á kwaanɩ á beeri bu wʋm jǝgǝ, sɩ á na nɛ-o, sɩ á joori á ba á ta á brɩ-nɩ, sɩ a dɩ vu a zul-o.»
translated: When the wise men had left Bethlehem, they were told: “Go and search carefully for the child, and when you have found him, report back to me so that I too may go and worship him.”

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: O na je da kʋntʋ tɩn, kɔ-fɔrɔ maa tui o te, yɩ ba jaanɩ yawɩɩna zanzan ba wǝli da ba tui. Badaara dɛɛn yɩ gwanɩ, badonnǝ maa yɩ lilwǝ dɩ kwaarʋ dɩ mɩmaarʋ, kʋ wǝli dɩ balʋ yawɩɩrʋ dwi dwi na jɩgɩ-ba tɩn. Ba maa jaanɩ-ba ba ve ba tiŋi Zezi yigǝ nɩ, yɩ o pa ba na yazurǝ.
translated: When they were sitting under the tree, a snake came out and bit one of them, so they all got up and ran away. The one who was bitten cried out, saying that the snake had bitten him on the leg, so they all ran off and left him there. They went and told Jesus what had happened, and he came and healed him.

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: Zezi daa ma ta o wɩ: «Wɛ paarɩ dɩm nyɩ dɩ nɔɔnʋ na kwe wo-duuru o du o kara wʋnɩ te tɩn mʋ.
translated: Then they said to him: “We have heard that you are the one who is able to heal this sickness that is killing people.”

ref: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
text: Dɛ dɩm kʋntʋ nɩ, Sadusɩan tiinǝ badonnǝ dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ ba vu Zezi te. Bantʋ mʋ brɩ nɩ, nɔɔnʋ na tɩga o daa bá joori o bi tɩn.
translated: When the time came, the Sadducees came to him and asked him a question about the resurrection. They said, “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man dies and leaves a wife but no children, his brother should marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.”

ref: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
text: «Nabiin-bu wʋm nan na joori o ba dɩ o paarɩ-zulǝ yam, yɩ Wɛ malɛsɩ sɩm maama tɔgɩ sɩ wʋ o tee nɩ, o laan wʋ́ ba o jǝni o paarɩ jangɔŋɔ kʋm baŋa nɩ.
translated: The prophet said that if someone wants to know whether he is truly a child of God, he should examine whether he loves his fellow believers.

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: Zezi na ve yigǝ fɩnfɩɩn tɩn, o daa ma na nɔɔna bale. Ba dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Zakɩ. O nyaanɩ dɩ maa wʋra, o yɩrɩ mʋ Zan. Ba ko yɩrɩ mʋ Zebede. Ba maa wʋ naboro wʋnɩ ba kwɛ ba bʋrɩ.
translated: They had four children, the first was called Nɔɔna Bale. The second was named Zakɩ. The third was named Zan. The fourth was named Zebede. They were all boys and they grew up to become hunters.
